payload.text:
Viết cho tôi một app chuyên viết bài cho nha khoa theo các nọi dung bài viết kiểu như thế này 

(
Để xây dựng nội dung cho một phòng khám nha khoa (website, fanpage, blog) hiệu quả, bạn cần đa dạng hóa các loại bài viết để tiếp cận khách hàng ở nhiều giai đoạn khác nhau (từ lúc tìm hiểu thông tin đến lúc quyết định điều trị).
Dưới đây là danh sách phân loại các bài viết chăm sóc cho nha khoa:
1. Nhóm bài viết về Dịch vụ (Service Pages)
Đây là nhóm bài quan trọng nhất để bán hàng. Bài viết cần chi tiết, thuyết phục và đầy đủ thông tin.
Giới thiệu tổng quan dịch vụ: Niềng răng, trồng răng Implant, bọc răng sứ, tẩy trắng răng, nhổ răng khôn...
Phân tích ưu/nhược điểm: So sánh các phương pháp (ví dụ: Niềng răng mắc cài kim loại vs. Invisalign; Răng sứ Cercon vs. Emax).
Quy trình thực hiện: Các bước khám và điều trị diễn ra như thế nào (giúp khách hàng bớt sợ hãi).
Đối tượng phù hợp: Dịch vụ này dành cho ai? (Hô, móm, mất răng, răng ố vàng...).
2. Nhóm bài viết về Bệnh lý & Triệu chứng (Educational/Symptom)
Nhắm vào khách hàng đang gặp vấn đề và tìm kiếm giải pháp trên Google (SEO).
Giải mã triệu chứng: Tại sao bị chảy máu chân răng? Ê buốt răng là dấu hiệu bệnh gì? Đau răng khôn nên làm gì?
Kiến thức bệnh lý: Sâu răng, viêm nha chu, viêm tủy, hôi miệng... (Nguyên nhân, hậu quả và cách phòng ngừa).
Hậu quả nếu không điều trị: Cảnh báo rủi ro (ví dụ: "Mất răng lâu ngày gây tiêu xương hàm như thế nào?").
3. Nhóm bài viết về Chi phí & Khuyến mãi (Cost & Promo)
Yếu tố quyết định lớn nhất đến hành vi khách hàng.
Bảng giá niêm yết: Công khai giá các dịch vụ.
Chính sách trả góp: Hướng dẫn thủ tục trả góp (rất quan trọng với niềng răng và Implant).
So sánh chi phí: Tại sao giá ở đây cao/thấp hơn chỗ khác? (Giải thích về chất lượng vật liệu, tay nghề bác sĩ).
Chương trình khuyến mãi: Giảm giá nhân dịp lễ, tết, sinh nhật phòng khám, check-in nhận quà.
4. Nhóm bài viết "Người thật việc thật" (Social Proof)
Xây dựng niềm tin (Trust) cho phòng khám.
Case Study (Ca lâm sàng): Phân tích ca điều trị khó, hình ảnh Before/After (Trước và Sau).
Feedback khách hàng: Bài phỏng vấn, trích dẫn lời khen, video cảm nhận của khách hàng đã sử dụng dịch vụ.
Nhật ký điều trị: Ví dụ: "Hành trình tháo niềng sau 2 năm của bạn A".
5. Nhóm bài viết Hướng dẫn chăm sóc tại nhà (Tips & Tricks)
Tạo giá trị cho cộng đồng và giữ chân khách hàng cũ.
Vệ sinh răng miệng: Cách chải răng đúng cách, cách dùng chỉ nha khoa, máy tăm nước.
Chế độ ăn uống: Nên ăn gì sau khi nhổ răng/trồng răng/niềng răng? Thực phẩm tốt cho men răng.
Mẹo vặt: Cách chữa đau răng tạm thời tại nhà, cách làm trắng răng tự nhiên (cần đính chính chuyên môn).
6. Nhóm bài viết về Thương hiệu & Đội ngũ (Brand Authority)
Khẳng định uy tín và cơ sở vật chất.
Profile bác sĩ: Giới thiệu bằng cấp, chứng chỉ, kinh nghiệm, số ca điều trị thành công.
Cơ sở vật chất: Giới thiệu máy móc, công nghệ (Máy CT Conebeam, máy quét dấu hàm iTero...).
Hoạt động phòng khám: Đội ngũ đi học nâng cao tay nghề, hoạt động từ thiện, team building.
7. Nhóm bài viết Hỏi đáp (Q&A)
Giải đáp nhanh các thắc mắc thường gặp.
FAQ: Tổng hợp các câu hỏi ngắn (Niềng răng có đau không? Tẩy trắng răng giữ được bao lâu?).
Góc chuyên gia: Bác sĩ trả lời trực tiếp các câu hỏi gửi về fanpage.
Phá bỏ lầm tưởng: Những quan niệm sai lầm về nha khoa (Ví dụ: "Lấy cao răng làm mòn men răng?").
8. Nhóm bài viết Nha khoa trẻ em (Pediatric Dentistry)
Nhắm vào đối tượng phụ huynh.
Độ tuổi thay răng: Lịch mọc răng và thay răng của bé.
Tiền chỉnh nha: Tại sao cần niềng răng sớm cho trẻ?
Tâm lý trẻ em: Làm sao để bé không sợ đi khám răng?
Mẹo nhỏ khi viết bài nha khoa:
Hình ảnh: Cần chân thực nhưng tránh dùng hình máu me gây sợ hãi (đặc biệt là hình phẫu thuật).
Ngôn ngữ: Chuyên môn cần được diễn giải đơn giản, dễ hiểu cho người bình thường.
CTA (Kêu gọi hành động): Luôn có nút đặt lịch hẹn hoặc tư vấn miễn phí ở cuối bài.
Chuẩn SEO: Nghiên cứu từ khóa người dùng hay tìm kiếm để đặt tiêu đề bài viết.
User

Có thêm một khung điền prompt lưu ý nữa. App sẽ viết hàng loạt số bài theo yêu cầu của tôi. Dùng gemini 3 pro

**Analyzing the Requirements**

I'm currently focused on the core purpose: crafting a React app for generating social media content plans for dentistry, all powered by the Gemini API's `gemini-3-pro-preview`. My thoughts are circling the user's specific need to develop a system with this functionality.


**Structuring the Content Plan**

I'm now prioritizing the precise Content Plan format and the distinct categories. My focus is crystallizing around how to translate the `gemini-3-pro-preview` model's output into the required table structure. I'm also ensuring all the required fields for each post are included, with the help of the user's input.


**Mapping Out Components**

I'm now deep-diving into the actual structure. I'm setting up the core parts: content plan item interfaces and API responses, the Gemini interaction service, a table to view the outcome, and a control panel for user input. I'm focusing on the component design, specifically `App.tsx`, `ControlPanel.tsx`, and `ContentTable.tsx`, and their state and layout.


**Defining the Gemini Service**

I'm now fully immersed in the details of the Gemini integration, specifically in `geminiService.ts`. I'm meticulously refining the prompt engineering strategy. It's critical to feed the AI context and the required output structure. I've decided to leverage JSON for easy parsing into the React table. I'm focusing on defining the response schema. I'm creating a schema for an array of objects that represent table rows.



**Constructing the Interface**

I'm presently building the `ContentItem` interface in TypeScript, to precisely model the content plan's data structure. This includes essential fields like topic, title, and content, along with columns for visuals and hashtags. My aim is to feed this interface to the Gemini API, alongside a structured prompt. I am also working on setting up the Gemini API connection, ready for model invocation and data retrieval. I'm focusing on defining this integration with the required API key for the Gemini model.


**Integrating User Feedback**

I'm now integrating feedback, especially the need for loading spinners, because I understand generating many posts takes time. I'm also focusing on graceful API error handling. I'm ensuring robust error checks throughout the integration. I've also just made adjustments to the core files. I'm establishing the structure for `metadata.json`, `index.html`, and ensuring Tailwind scripts are included.


**Defining Custom Fields**

I've just added a "Custom Note" field to the prompt, as per the user's instructions. This will allow for tailored content generation. I'm also ensuring the API key is being accessed correctly from the environment variables, and confirming the use of the `gemini-3-pro-preview` model. Finally, the UI design is now centered around a "Dental Blue" aesthetic, for a cohesive user experience.


**Specifying Data Structures**

I'm presently building the `ContentItem` interface in TypeScript, to precisely model the content plan's data structure. It's an array of objects, with properties like `purpose`, `topic`, `note`, `title`, `detailedContent`, `visualDescription`, `designBrief`, and `hashtags`. I'm also preparing for the imports, focusing on `@google/genai`, and ensuring the code is not using any deprecated API calls. I also have to check the use of React hooks. Finally, I'm organizing the prompts for the Gemini API, I'm storing them in a constant string to keep the service file concise.

entries.description: App metadata definition
HTML entry point with Tailwind CSS
React Root entry point
TypeScript definitions for the application
Service to interact with Google Gemini API
Component for user inputs
Component for displaying the content plan table
Main application component

hãy viết các thông số điều chỉnh. ví dụ như % video % hình ảnh % hình thiết kế. Nói chung là đủ thông số

đây là chân của mỗi bài viết

Ngoài ra còn có xuất ra file excel

**Refining AI Parameters**

I'm currently focused on the adjustable parameters for the Dental Content Planner. I've successfully incorporated the percentage controls for videos/images and designed/real images. My next steps involve determining the most effective range and granularity for each parameter to provide optimal content variation for the user. I'm also considering how these adjustments impact overall content quality and relevance.


**Implementing Footer Integration**

I've determined that a consistent footer/signature needs to be implemented across all content. I am leaning towards programmatically appending the standardized signature to the AI-generated content to ensure consistency and efficient token usage. I'm currently working to integrate this into the content post generation process. Next, I'll export to Excel.


**Incorporating New Sliders**

I've just added the new fields, `videoRatio` and `designRatio`, to `GeneratorSettings`. I'm now integrating sliders for these ratios into the input panel for user control. I'm thinking of grouping these under "Advanced Settings" for clarity. I've also decided to append the signature in the frontend post-generation. This is more efficient and safe.


**Planning Excel Export**

I'm now focusing on Excel export functionality. I've chosen to use `xlsx` from `esm.sh` to create proper `.xlsx` files, avoiding heavy dependencies. I'll need to add an "Export Excel" button and I'm deciding where to put it: either in `App.tsx` above the table or inside `PlanTable` itself.


**Designing New Sliders**

I'm implementing two new sliders for user control: one for video/image ratio and another for visual style (design vs. real photos). The video ratio slider will range from 100% images to 100% videos, while the visual style slider will range from 100% real photos to 100% design images. I've also decided to handle the user-provided signature by appending it in the Gemini service for full text delivery to the UI. I will add the new settings to `GeneratorSettings` in `types.ts`.


**Refining Implementation Steps**

I'm now in the process of implementing the changes across multiple files. I've updated `index.html` to include `xlsx` and `types.ts` with new settings fields. Sliders have been added to `components/InputPanel.tsx`, and I've also updated `services/geminiService.ts` to use new settings and append the footer. I'm working now on incorporating Excel export functionality in `App.tsx`. I will adjust the video/image and design percentages in settings.


**Enhancing Content Generation**

I'm now integrating Vietnamese text for the "Advanced Settings" section in `InputPanel.tsx`, adding the "Cấu hình nâng cao" label. In `geminiService.ts`, I'm developing a method to calculate the video/image counts from percentages to guide the model's generation, using phrases like "Tỷ lệ Video mong muốn: X%." Also, I'm verifying the `xlsx` import.

To address your request, I will update the application with the following changes:

1.  **Add Advanced Configuration**: Add sliders to adjust the percentage of Video vs. Images and Designed Images vs. Real Photos.
2.  **Auto-Append Footer**: Automatically add the specific "Nha Khoa ADORA" signature to every generated post.
3.  **Excel Export**: Add a button to export the content plan to an Excel (.xlsx) file.

Here are the changes:

entries.description: Add xlsx library to import map for Excel export
Add new configuration settings for video and design ratios
Implement ratio instructions in prompt and append footer to content
Add sliders for video and design percentage inputs
Add Excel export functionality and update service call

payload.text: Hãy thêm phần điều chỉnh các thông số để lịch hợp lý. Bạn hãy tự nghĩ ra các thông số, tôi cần các bài viết đa dạng nên đừng áp thôgn số quá

**Developing Adjustable Parameters**

I'm currently focused on identifying parameters that can make the content plan more reasonable and diverse. The user wants more control over the application's outputs, so I'm aiming for flexibility and nuanced adjustments. I'm prioritizing parameters that can significantly alter the content's tone, scope, and target audience.


**Incorporating User Preferences**

I'm now thinking about how to integrate the user's need for "diverse" outputs directly into the adjustable parameters. I'm leaning toward allowing randomness within the parameters themselves to ensure variety. Specifically, I've outlined parameters for "Tone of Voice" and "Target Audience" to make the content plan more reasonable. The current parameters are: Category, Count, Video %, and Design %. I plan to add more to enhance the social media content plan.


**Expanding Parameter Control**

I've been working on expanding the parameter control, specifically focusing on introducing more flexibility. I've added Post Length (Short, Medium, Long, Mixed) and Call to Action Intensity (Soft, Hard, Mixed) to the framework. I'm also planning a "Diversity Configuration" section with dropdown options for Tone, Length, and Target Audience. This approach aims for balanced and versatile content, allowing the AI to offer diverse outputs, with an "Auto/Mixed" option for the best default.


**Prioritizing Content Flexibility**

I'm now prioritizing the parameters for "Content Style/Vibe," which will include "tone," "targetAudience," and "postLength," as the user seems to want them, to keep the content flexible. I'm focusing on updating `types.ts`, `InputPanel.tsx`, and `geminiService.ts` to implement these updates. I am also updating the prompt to tell Gemini to prioritize the style, but not strictly. The user doesn't want parameters to be forced.


**Refining Mixed Parameter Logic**

I'm now focusing on how the "Mixed" options for tone, target audience, and post length should be handled within the prompt. I'm updating `geminiService.ts` to implement specific instructions when "Mixed" is selected, guiding the AI to use diverse styles and lengths based on the content's topic. Specifically, I am updating the prompt logic. I'm aiming for flexibility and nuanced adjustments within the application's functionality. I am using the files `types.ts`, `components/InputPanel.tsx`, and `services/geminiService.ts` to implement these updates.


**Adding Content Control**

I'm now integrating the new dropdowns for tone, target audience, and post length into the input panel, updating `InputPanel.tsx`. I've added a crucial instruction to the prompt: "LƯU Ý QUAN TRỌNG: Các thông số trên là định hướng chung. Hãy linh hoạt điều chỉnh cho phù hợp với từng chủ đề cụ thể để nội dung không bị rập khuôn." This aims for flexible parameter usage. I decided that using enums in `types.ts` is unnecessary for this context, and string literals are a fine choice.


**Updating Default Parameters**

I'm now setting the default parameters to the "Mixed/Flexible" options for the best variety. I've updated the implementation plan to include the addition of tone, targetAudience, and postLength to GeneratorSettings in types.ts. I'm also adding select boxes for these new options in InputPanel.tsx and mapping these settings to the prompt in geminiService.ts. My goal is to maximize flexibility.

Based on your request to add more adjustment parameters while keeping the content diverse and "reasonable" (not too rigid), I will add 3 new configuration options: **Tone of Voice** (Giọng văn), **Target Audience** (Đối tượng khách hàng), and **Post Length** (Độ dài bài viết).

I will set the default values to "Flexible/Mixed" so that the AI automatically diversifies the content, but you can force specific styles if needed.

Here are the changes:

entries.description: Add new diversity settings to GeneratorSettings
Add UI controls for Tone, Audience, and Length
Update prompt to include new diversity settings
Update initial state with new settings

payload.text: Hãy có phần điều chỉnh để có thể thay đổi % các mục đíhc bài viết

**Revising Distribution Strategy**

I'm thinking about how to improve the distribution strategy of content categories. Currently, it's either a single category or "ALL," with the model then distributing it. I'm exploring allowing the user to set explicit percentages for each category. That's a new approach to consider.


**Expanding Control Options**

Now, I'm diving deeper into the technical implementation. I'm adding a type for the distribution map in `types.ts` and updating `GeneratorSettings` to accommodate a `purposeDistribution` field. On the UI side, I'm thinking about how to visualize the user's choices. Instead of individual sliders, I'm leaning toward a set of controls that sum to 100%, or maybe simplified weights. This aims for granular control without overwhelming the interface.


**Refining UI Implementation**

I'm now zeroing in on UI implementation details. While sliders seem appropriate for percentages, I'm reconsidering whether strictly enforcing a 100% sum is ideal. Weights or allowing approximate breakdowns might offer a better user experience, as would simpler sliders for frequency. I've decided to implement a "Custom Distribution" toggle that reveals input fields for categories, dynamically enabling it when the distribution is set to "ALL". To avoid overwhelming the user, especially with 8 categories, I'll make the "Purpose Distribution" section collapsible. Also, I'll create a UI-friendly mapping for those long `ContentCategory` enum strings. I'm also planning some modifications to the `generateContentPlan` function in `services/geminiService.ts`.


**Adjusting Percentage Implementation**

Now I'm designing the input elements for the category percentages. I'll use a dynamic list of sliders for eight categories, allowing users to specify approximate target percentages. If the user selects the "ALL" category, a button or accordion will reveal the "Configure post ratios" section. Inside, percentage input fields will be displayed, offering a more user-friendly way to adjust the weights for content distribution. I'm taking the "approximate targets" route for the prompt to the AI, to make it not too rigid.


**Prioritizing Category Distribution**

I'm now zeroing in on defining the categories. I'm adding a section called "Content Distribution" with sliders for each. I'm modifying `types.ts` with `purposeDistribution: Record<string, number>`. The default will be "Auto/Balanced." If the user adjusts the sliders, those values will be passed. The `ContentCategory` enum strings are long, so I'll need a helper function to shorten them for display. I need to make the UI friendly, to avoid lots of sliders, so a "Priority" selector for each group is something to explore as a simpler option.



**Defining Weights and Sliders**

I'm now determining how to render the input fields. I plan to use sliders for all categories, excluding "ALL." These sliders will represent "weights" rather than strictly enforced percentages. The sliders will range from 0 to 100, and I'll include these weights in the prompt as relative priorities. The default value will be 0, allowing the AI to automatically decide the distribution if the user doesn't specify any weights.


**Refining Percentage Inputs**

I'm now implementing simple number inputs for percentages next to each category label in the UI, as the user requested percentage values. I've updated `types.ts` to include `purposeWeights` and added distribution controls in `InputPanel.tsx`, which are hidden behind a toggle. I'm also working on generating the prompt string in `geminiService.ts` based on these weights, which includes filtering out zero weights and constructing the prompt in Vietnamese. I'm also refining the UI, aiming to improve the layout of the percentage input fields.


**Constructing Category Inputs**

I'm now implementing the UI for category-specific percentage inputs. I'm focusing on the condition where the "ALL" category is selected. I'll make a section "Tỷ trọng nội dung (Khi chọn 'Tất cả')" visible in that case. The logic in `geminiService.ts` will generate a prompt string based on the `settings.purposeWeights`. In `types.ts`, I'll define `purposeWeights` to map from `ContentCategory` enum strings to numbers, and create a list of `ContentCategory` values. I'll initialize these to empty or equal to avoid immediate feedback.


**Finalizing Percentage Implementation**

I'm making progress on finalizing the percentage implementation and have almost completed the UI for the category-specific input fields. I've updated the `types.ts` file by adding `CATEGORY_LIST` and updating `GeneratorSettings` to contain `purposeWeights`. I've also initialized `purposeWeights` in `App.tsx`. In `InputPanel.tsx`, I've added the UI elements for the inputs. Finally, I'm updating the prompt string generation in `geminiService.ts`, ensuring it reflects the user's approximate percentage inputs. The UI will use sliders with values from 0-100%, and the system will prompt the AI with "Approximate %" to allow for flexibility even if the sum exceeds 100%. The prompt will be constructed in Vietnamese, reflecting desired ratios.

To fulfill your request to add a section for adjusting the percentages of different content purposes (categories), I will update the application to include a "Content Distribution" configuration.

Here is the plan:
1.  **Update `types.ts`**: Define the structure for storing purpose weights.
2.  **Update `App.tsx`**: Initialize the default weights.
3.  **Update `InputPanel.tsx`**: Add a collapsible section with sliders for each content category, allowing you to set the desired percentage for each.
4.  **Update `geminiService.ts`**: Incorporate these percentages into the prompt sent to Gemini.

**Developing Knowledge Integration**

I'm working on the "Knowledge" input field, aiming to embed dental expertise into the application. My goal is to prevent the AI from generating inaccurate information. This added field should allow users to feed in specific dental facts, thereby improving the AI's reliability and output. I'm adjusting the input panel for better usability. I've been experimenting with layouts to accommodate the new "Knowledge Base" textarea. I am leaning towards: Row 1: Category (6), Count (6); Row 2: Custom Prompt (6), Knowledge Base (6) for a clean presentation.


**Finalizing Input Structure**

I'm now implementing the new layout for the input panel. I've opted for a full-width input for "Dental Knowledge" beneath the existing fields, as requested. The "Knowledge Base" field will allow the user to provide critical background information for better generation. I'm also updating `geminiService.ts` to integrate this new knowledge base into prompt construction. I will ensure it enforces adherence to the provided dental information. I will be updating `types.ts` to include the new field.

I'm now making final layout adjustments in `InputPanel.tsx` to best integrate the "Knowledge Base" input. I'm focusing on user experience, and I've decided on a layout that prioritizes readability and ease of use, with a full-width "Knowledge Base" field below the existing inputs. I'll ensure that the new field will take up less vertical space in the application.


**Finalizing Prompt Integration**

I'm now incorporating the knowledge base data into the prompt construction within `geminiService.ts`. I'm including the "Knowledge Base" content in the prompt, emphasizing its mandatory nature to the AI to ensure accurate dental information is used. I'm focusing on ensuring it enforces adherence to the provided dental information. I will be updating `types.ts` to include the new field.



**Optimizing UI Space**

I'm now revising the layout of the `InputPanel`. I've decided to keep the existing structure, adding the "Knowledge Base" field below the existing inputs. The new layout will be implemented using the existing structure and incorporating a full-width "Knowledge Base" textarea in the second row. I've updated `types.ts` to include the `knowledgeBase` field, and I've initialized it within the app's default state. I am now adding a new textarea in `InputPanel.tsx`. I've updated the placeholder to reflect this intent, setting an example input of "VD: Quy trình Implant tại phòng khám gồm 4 bước: ..., Giá niềng răng từ 20tr - 40tr,...". I'm also preparing to update the prompt in `geminiService.ts` to accommodate the knowledge base. Finally, I'll integrate the new code.
